engine_heritage: Creation Engine 2 (evolved from Gamebryo/NetImmerse + id Tech networking + Havok physics + Scaleform UI)

bcstelemetry.purpose: Bethesda Cloud Services telemetry - game analytics, metrics collection, event factory

bps.purpose: Bethesda Platform Services - lobby management, matchmaking, ping, platform config, push notifications (Pushy)

bsanimation.purpose: Animation engine - Havok behavior graph management, ragdoll physics, animation caching, sub-behavior graphs

bsaudio.purpose: Audio engine - XAudio2 backend, null audio impl, game sound management, client-side audio

bschromium.purpose: Embedded Chromium (CEF) - in-game web browser for Atom Shop, news, and web content rendering

bscore.purpose: Core engine foundation - memory manager, threading (Win32), message bus, compacting memory store

bsdevices.purpose: Input system - device abstraction, input events, control mapping, rumble/haptics, enable layers

bsdiag.purpose: Diagnostics - exception/crash handling, stall detection, HTML callback hashing, JSON metadata for crash reports

bsgraphics.purpose: Graphics/rendering - DirectX renderer, texture data management

bshavok.purpose: Havok physics - collision detection, rigid bodies, constraints, character controllers, world simulation

bshavokmigration.purpose: Havok migration layer - version compatibility wrappers for physics data (82 files = massive migration effort)

bsmain.purpose: Main application - game loop, client/server initialization, state machine, platform entry points

bsmenu.purpose: Menu/UI framework - Scaleform/Flash integration, interface ACL (access control), menu state management

bsmovie.purpose: Video playback - Bink video decoder integration

bsmusic.purpose: Music system - dynamic music management and transitions

bspathfinding.purpose: AI pathfinding - navmesh generation/queries, path requests, door handling, obstacle avoidance

bsplatform.purpose: Platform abstraction layer - file I/O, system info, WinSock networking, cloud storage integration

bsresource.purpose: Resource management - BA2 archive streaming, async asset loading, location/path resolution

bsscript.purpose: Papyrus scripting - VM, compiler, linker, object binding, save/load, internal functions, profiling

bsshader.purpose: Shader pipeline - HLSL compilation, material system, render techniques, shader properties, lighting

bssystem.purpose: System utilities - crash dump generation, telemetry hooks, system-level integration

bssystemmonitor.purpose: Performance monitoring - system resource tracking and alerts

bsvm.purpose: Lua VM integration - lightweight scripting for internal tools, debug automation, config scripting

bswebserver.purpose: Embedded HTTP server - dev/QA debug web interface, remote console, performance dashboards

facegen.purpose: FaceGen - procedural face generation, morph targets, face texture management

game.purpose: Game logic - ALL gameplay: combat, AI, quests, inventory, world cells, NPCs, crafting, events, nuclear winter, workshops, perks, mutations, power armor, public events, daily ops, seasons, etc.

gamebryo.purpose: Gamebryo/NetImmerse engine - scene graph, 3D geometry, rendering pipeline, object hierarchy, transforms

havok.purpose: Havok SDK root - physics middleware integration point

idcoresocket.purpose: id Software socket library - low-level BSD socket abstraction for networking

idnet.purpose: id Software networking stack - reliable UDP protocol, delta snapshots, client/server state sync, bandwidth management

logging.purpose: Logging infrastructure - centralized log output and routing

scaleform.purpose: Scaleform GFx - Flash/ActionScript UI middleware integration

sdk.purpose: SDK/tooling - external SDK integration, tool support files

shared.purpose: Shared client/server code - network message definitions, entity components, data serialization, game constants

bsvm_lua.analysis: Only 1 visible source file in the dev build. Lua VM is used for internal tooling, automated testing (see AutomatedTesting:: messages), debug scripting, and possibly config parsing. Separate from the Papyrus gameplay scripting VM. The AutomatedTesting::ExecuteBatchedConsoleCommands message suggests Lua drives test automation.

bswebserver.analysis: 2 source files = lightweight HTTP server. Dev strings show http://127.0.0.1:8004/dashboard/services/season/details/commands endpoint. Used for: remote console commands, season service management, debug dashboards, QA automation. http://remoteviewer.bgs.local suggests a remote game state viewer for internal use.

bsmenu_interfaceacl.analysis: Interface ACL (Access Control List) in the menu system controls which UI elements are available. Gates debug menus, admin panels, and context-sensitive UI. Likely checks build type (dev/qa/retail) and user permissions to show/hide menu options. The 18 source files include Scaleform bindings for Flash UI.

bcstelemetry.analysis: Bethesda Cloud Services telemetry. factory.cpp = factory pattern for telemetry events. Based on dev strings, telemetry covers: player behavior, combat stats, economy transactions, session timing, crash data, mod usage (bnet::Mods::Telemetry::SendCollectionEnableEvent), performance metrics. Data flows to Bethesda's analytics backend.

idnet_architecture.analysis: 29 files from id Software's networking stack. This is the foundation of FO76's multiplayer - reliable UDP with delta compression snapshots. Key concepts: snapshots (world state), client prediction, server authority, bandwidth throttling. The 'idNetNetworkDiagnostics' message handler confirms runtime network diagnostics capability.
